name: TuHURA Biosciences, Inc./NV
business: TuHURA Biosciences is a phase 3 clinical stage immuno-oncology company developing novel therapeutics to overcome resistance to cancer immunotherapies. Its lead product candidate, IFx-2.0, is an innate immune agonist designed to overcome primary resistance to checkpoint inhibitors. The company also has a VISTA-inhibiting antibody, TBS-2025, and a Delta Opioid Receptor (DOR) technology for developing bi-specific, bi-functional antibody-drug conjugates.
sellsTo: patients with advanced or metastatic Merkel cell carcinoma
patients with relapsed/refractory mut NPM1 acute myeloid leukemia

exposures.sentence: We are a phase 3 clinical stage immuno-oncology company with three distinct technologies focused on the development of novel therapeutics designed to overcome primary and acquired resistance to cancer immunotherapies.

catalysts.event: Initiated Phase 3 trial for IFx-2.0 in Merkel cell carcinoma
catalysts.sentence: In June 2025, we initiated a single randomized placebo-controlled Phase 3 registration trial of administered as an adjunctive therapy to Keytruda (pembrolizumab) in first line treatment for patients with advanced or metastatic Merkel cell carcinoma who are checkpoint inhibitor na ve utilizing the FDA s accelerated approval pathway.

catalysts.event: FDA discussion for TBS-2025 development plans
catalysts.sentence: The Company currently plans on discussing its development plans with the FDA late in the first half of 2026 and initiating the planned Phase1b/2 trial in as early as the second half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Phase 3 enrollment completion and top-line data availability
catalysts.sentence: We anticipate that enrollment for the Phase 3 will take approximately 18 24 months from the initiation of the trial, with top-line data potentially being available 6 to 7 months following the last patient enrolled.
